mutation_pipeline: OperationIntent -> PatchPlan -> Review/Policy -> Apply -> Verify -> AuditReceipt

families.title: Reading and navigation
families.description: Open PDFs, inspect them safely, navigate page structure, and keep malformed documents in the safe-open path.

families.title: Search and text extraction
families.description: Deterministic substring search, reading-order hints, and text extraction evidence.

families.title: Rendering and images
families.description: Tile rendering, image XObjects, thumbnailing and visual comparison evidence.

families.title: Fonts and color
families.description: Font diagnostics, fallback handling, color fidelity and prepress reporting.

families.title: Metadata and preflight
families.description: XMP metadata inspection, preflight policy checks and scrub-planning claims.

families.title: Annotations and comments
families.description: Annotation objects, review comments and collaboration sidecars.

families.title: Forms and active content
families.description: AcroForms, XFA warnings, JavaScript restrictions and other active-content policy gates.

families.title: Signatures, encryption and permissions
families.description: Signed-document compatibility, encryption, permission bits and signature-preservation limits.

families.title: Attachments, portfolios, layers and multimedia
families.description: Embedded files, portfolios, optional content groups and rich-media limits.

families.title: Tagged PDFs, accessibility and standards
families.description: Tagged structure, reading order, accessibility, PDF/A, PDF/UA, PDF/X and PDF 2.0 claims.

families.title: Redaction
families.description: Sanitizing rewrite, leak verification and redaction receipts.

families.title: Page organization
families.description: Page insert, delete, reorder, rotate and split/merge planning through the mutation pipeline.

families.title: Optimization and linearization
families.description: Safe optimization, compression, dedupe, linearization and savings receipts.

families.title: Repair and incremental updates
families.description: Safe-open, repair planning, incremental revision handling and recovery receipts.

families.title: OCR and searchable PDFs
families.description: OCR-assisted searchable-PDF workflows and provider-gated extraction.

families.title: Conversion, printing and export
families.description: Source conversion, print/export and adapter-backed output claims.

claims.public_claim: Open and inspect generated baseline PDFs.
limitations: This is a baseline reader claim, not a guarantee for every PDF class.

claims.public_claim: Treat malformed PDFs as expected input and report parser diagnostics without mutating bytes.
limitations: Safe-open is diagnostic-first and does not promise successful repair.

claims.public_claim: Navigation, outlines and page-jump behavior remain tracked as a governed baseline rather than a marketing promise.
limitations: Navigation and outline fidelity remain adapter-owned and may vary by surface.

claims.public_claim: Navigate pages, zoom, fit, rotate, inspect thumbnails and restore the current reader session for the baseline PDF experience.
limitations: Navigation surfaces remain limited to the baseline reader workflow until broader UI automation lands.

claims.public_claim: Build deterministic text/search evidence for simple generated PDFs.
limitations: Complex glyph shaping, OCR and missing ToUnicode cases remain documented corpus placeholders.

claims.public_claim: Surface deterministic text extraction diagnostics, missing ToUnicode warnings and geometry fallbacks for search and accessibility workflows.
limitations: CJK, RTL and ligature shaping remain corpus-dependent until expanded text fixtures and oracle coverage land.

claims.public_claim: Text extraction preserves page geometry, reading order hints and safe diagnostics.
limitations: Geometry and ordering claims remain bounded by corpus fixtures and parser adapters.

claims.public_claim: Run deterministic render smoke and visual regression comparison for the current baseline renderer.
limitations: Production renderer parity must remain a post-launch claim until reference-renderer oracle results are accepted.

claims.public_claim: Provide keyboard and screen-reader accessibility reports for the reader baseline and tagged-PDF inspection workflows.
limitations: Tagged-PDF and PDF/UA oracle coverage remains limited to available local adapters and documented limitations.

claims.public_claim: Image XObjects and image-heavy PDFs are tracked through the renderer and corpus-governance boundaries.
limitations: Image fidelity claims need corpus fixtures and renderer comparisons before they become public promises.

claims.public_claim: Font diagnostics surface missing embeds, substitutions and extraction risks.
limitations: Font fidelity claims remain evidence-backed, not universal.

claims.public_claim: Inspect and plan metadata and XMP changes with explicit scrub policies.
limitations: Standards-grade PDF/A, PDF/UA and PDF/X conformance claims require external oracle reports before public parity claims.

claims.public_claim: Color fidelity, output intents and prepress diagnostics are oracle-backed rather than assumed.
limitations: Claims beyond diagnostics require differential oracle results and standards validation.

claims.public_claim: Plan annotation operations through core contracts without bypassing policy.
limitations: Editable annotation preservation and renderer/UI round trips need additional fixtures before public parity claims.

claims.public_claim: Review comments stay local-first and plan-only until the packet workflow is fully proven.
limitations: Comment round-trips do not imply mutating import is permitted.

claims.public_claim: Plan AcroForm fill and flatten operations with policy-visible write modes.
limitations: XFA and JavaScript execution remain disabled or warning-only until separately approved.

claims.public_claim: JavaScript, launch actions, RichMedia and embedded executables are warned on or blocked by default.
limitations: Unsafe active content is not a public capability target.

claims.public_claim: Signed-document compatibility is tracked as a documented limitation.
limitations: Signature preservation, validation and signing are not public baseline claims yet.

claims.public_claim: Encryption, permission bits and policy-aware opening remain tracked without overclaiming support.
limitations: Permission enforcement and cryptographic compatibility depend on adapter/runtime evidence.

claims.public_claim: Portfolio and attachment workflows remain documented limitations.
limitations: Portfolio extraction, attachment preservation and embedded-file mutation are not public parity claims yet.

claims.public_claim: Optional content groups and rich media are tracked as compatibility gaps, not as stable support claims.
limitations: Layer and multimedia workflows require separate corpus and policy evidence.

claims.public_claim: Tagged PDFs, accessibility and reading-order evidence stay bound to explicit fixtures and audits.
limitations: Accessibility claims need surface-specific audit evidence before they can be called mature.

claims.public_claim: PDF/A, PDF/UA, PDF/X and PDF 2.0 remain oracle-backed standards claims.
limitations: Standards-grade conformance claims require external oracle reports before public parity claims.

claims.public_claim: Secure redaction requires sanitizing rewrite and verification receipts.
limitations: No public claim should imply applied redaction is complete until rewritten output is verified.

claims.public_claim: Page operations are planned through the mutation pipeline without auto-approval.
limitations: Apply/save paths and UI page organization remain gated until transaction apply and audit receipts are end-to-end verified.

claims.public_claim: Optimization, compression and linearization stay oracle-backed and review-gated.
limitations: Optimization claims require before/after receipts and differential evidence.

claims.public_claim: Repair planning and incremental update handling stay inside the safe-open and recovery model.
limitations: Repair output must preserve receipts and may invalidate signatures.

claims.public_claim: OCR and searchable-PDF workflows remain provider-gated and opt-in.
limitations: OCR remains provider-gated and should not be marketed as a baseline feature.

claims.public_claim: Keep OCR-backed searchable-PDF planning provider-gated and opt-in until quality evidence lands.
limitations: Cloud OCR is not enabled by default and local OCR remains a later provider-gated workflow.

claims.public_claim: Conversion and source-pipeline planning expose capability discovery and clear failure modes.
limitations: External converters require provider discovery, policy approval and fixtures before output fidelity claims.

claims.public_claim: Printing and export remain adapter-owned claims tied to platform and release evidence.
limitations: Print/export behavior varies by adapter and platform capability.
